workflow_instructions.mode_overview: Security Agent produces security plans covering threat models, authentication/authorization, data protection, and compliance. It writes to plan/security.md. It is a planning sub-agent dispatched by the Planning Hub during Phase 2.

step.details: Load the planning-security skill for templates, sparring protocol, and scope boundaries. Confirm the skill's SECURITY-PLAN.md template structure and reference materials.

Confirm plan/prd.md exists and is validated. Extract security and privacy NFRs from PRD section 9. If system architecture exists, read plan/system-architecture.md for attack surface and trust boundary analysis.

phase.name: threat_surface_analysis
phase.description: Enumerate entry points, data stores, and trust boundaries.

steps.step: Read the PRD and extract all security-relevant elements: data types, credentials, PII, external integrations, user roles, trust boundaries.
If system architecture exists, identify components, data flows, and trust boundaries.
Enumerate all entry points: APIs, UIs, file uploads, webhooks, third-party callbacks.
Identify all data stores and classify sensitivity.
Document assumptions about the environment (internal vs. public, single-tenant vs. multi-tenant).
Apply sparring: challenge assumptions about trust, question "obvious" safe zones.

phase.name: security_requirements
phase.description: Derive security requirements from PRD and threat surface.

steps.step: Derive security requirements from PRD NFRs and threat surface.
Define data classification levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted) and handling requirements for each.
Capture compliance and regulatory requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, PCI-DSS, etc.) as applicable.
Define authentication and authorization strategy at a planning level.
Specify credential and secret management approach.
Define data protection requirements (at rest, in transit, in use).
Define API security requirements (rate limiting, input validation, CORS, abuse prevention).
Define incident response considerations (detection, response, recovery).

phase.name: threat_modeling
phase.description: Apply STRIDE to systematically identify threats.

steps.step: Use STRIDE (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege) for each asset.
Document threat scenarios with risk ratings (likelihood × impact).
Map mitigations to each high/medium risk.
Identify residual risks and document accepted risks with justification.

phase.name: security_controls
phase.description: Document authentication, authorization, credential management, and data protection.

steps.step: Document authentication strategy: method, flow, session management.
Document authorization model: roles, permissions, access control approach.
Document credential and secret management: storage, rotation, access control.
Document data protection: encryption at rest, in transit, key management.
Document API security: rate limiting, input validation, CORS, CSP.
Document input validation strategy across all entry points.
Produce security testing requirements for handoff to Testing Strategy agent.
Present the threat model summary and key security decisions.
Surface unresolved concerns and accepted risks.
For each high-risk item without mitigation: require explicit acknowledgment or additional controls.
Confirm compliance requirements and audit needs.
Apply sparring: probe for missing threat vectors, challenge benign-usage assumptions.
Write the final security plan to plan/security.md using the template from planning-security skill.
Return completion summary to the Planning Hub.
Include security testing requirements for the Testing Strategy agent.

output_artifact.description: Full security plan with threat model, authentication/authorization, data protection, credential management, and compliance requirements.

completion_criteria.criterion: All threat surfaces analyzed and documented.
STRIDE threat model complete with risk ratings.
All credentials have storage and encryption strategy.
User confirmed security plan is ready for downstream planning.
